title: Universal Analytical Design for Perfect Chiral Circulation in Arbitrary Rings: Triangular Realizations via Floquet and Anyonic Physics
authors: Chaorong Guo, Hongzheng Wu, Zenong Zhou, Ai-Xi Chen, Xiaobing Luo
abstract: Perfect chiral circulation-the sequential transfer of a quantum state around a closed loop with unit fidelity-has been achieved in specific few-site systems, yet a general theory remains absent. We prove that for translationally invariant $N$-site rings, an equidistant energy spectrum is the necessary and sufficient condition for such circulation, and we derive an exact closed-form Hamiltonian valid for arbitrary $N$. In the minimal three-site ring, we demonstrate two physically distinct realizations: Floquet engineering in a driven open chain, and correlated doublon dynamics in an anyon-Hubbard model where fractional statistics intrinsically provide the requisite gauge flux. Grounded in this universal spectral criterion and an explicit Hamiltonian construction, we provide an analytical, transparent design framework for perfect chiral circulation applicable to diverse platforms, such as superconducting circuits, classical electrical circuits, and photonic synthetic dimensions.
summary: The paper presents a universal theory for achieving perfect, unidirectional quantum state transfer around any N-site ring by designing a Hamiltonian with an equidistant energy spectrum. It demonstrates that this can be physically realized using Floquet engineering in driven chains or through the intrinsic statistics of anyons. This provides a robust, analytical framework for creating quantum routers in diverse experimental platforms.
main_problem: Establishing a universal analytical design for perfect chiral circulation—the sequential transfer of a quantum state around an N-site ring with unit fidelity—and overcoming the lack of a general theory for rings larger than three sites.
main_result: The authors prove that an equidistant energy spectrum is the necessary and sufficient condition for perfect chiral circulation in translationally invariant rings and provide an exact closed-form Hamiltonian construction for arbitrary N.
method: The study uses spectral analysis of the evolution operator, Magnus expansion for Floquet engineering, and second-order perturbation theory for anyonic doublon dynamics to derive and validate the required Hamiltonian parameters.
model_or_system: The model consists of translationally invariant N-site rings with periodic boundary conditions, specifically realized through Floquet-engineered driven open chains and anyon-Hubbard models involving correlated doublon dynamics.
key_observables: Site populations (Pn(t)), average transfer fidelity (F_bar), energy spectrum, and group velocity.
important_parameters: Number of sites (N), total synthetic flux (Phi), hopping amplitudes (J), driving frequency (omega), drive amplitude (A), drive phase (phi), and anyonic statistical angle (theta).
main_assumptions: The Floquet analysis assumes the high-frequency limit (J << omega), and the anyonic model assumes the strong-interaction regime (U >> J).
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows a schematic of the N=3 chiral ring and state transfer direction; Figure 5 compares numerical results of the driven Hamiltonian against analytical effective models.
paper_structure: The paper begins by establishing the universal spectral criterion for chiral circulation, then provides the analytical Hamiltonian construction, followed by specific physical realizations via Floquet engineering and anyonic physics, and concludes with scalability and robustness tests.
why_interesting: This work provides a deterministic 'quantum router' design that is highly relevant for quantum information processing, offering a scalable framework for unidirectional state transfer in various platforms like superconducting circuits and photonic synthetic dimensions.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Minimal chiral ring (N = 3). (Left) Triangular ring model with uniform hopping amplitude J and hopping phase ϕ per bond, yielding a total synthetic flux of Φ = 3ϕ. (Right) The chiral excitation circulation is indicated by text and arrows as |1⟩→|2⟩→|3⟩→|1⟩at Φ = π/2.
FIG. 2. Dynamics and spectrum for N = 3. (a) Clockwise circulation (Φ = π/2). Site populations Pn(t) = |⟨n|ψ(t)⟩|2
FIG. 3. Scalable chiral dynamics (N = 4, 5). (a) Connec- tivity schematics for rings with N = 4 and N = 5. Red solid arrows: NN hopping; blue dashed arrows: NNN hop- ping. For N = 4: J/JNNN = √

FIG. 5. (a) Floquet synthesis of chiral transport. Asymmet- ric phase-shifted periodic driving converts an open three-site chain (left) into an effective triangular model with uniform coupling Jeff and chiral flux Φeff (right). (b) Clockwise cir- culation (φ = π/3). Solid curves show the exact numerical results from the driven Hamiltonian (6); markers show the an- alytical results from ˆHeff in Eq. (7). (c) Counterclockwise cir- culation (φ = −π/3). Parameters are ω/J = 40, A/ω ≈2.37. The initial state is |1⟩. Time is in units of 1/J.

FIG. 4. Scalability and robustness. (a) Chiral circulation for N = 10. The Hamiltonian includes all-to-all couplings given by Eqs. (4a) and (4b). (b) Average transfer fidelity ¯F versus on-site disorder W/ ¯J for N = 3, 5, 10. (c) ¯F versus hopping disorder δJ/ ¯J. Here, W and δJ denote the maximum on-site and hopping disorder strengths. For each site and bond, the disorder is randomly and uniformly sampled from [−W, W] and [−δJ, δJ], respectively, and scaled by the mean hopping amplitude ¯J. Results are averaged over 300 disorder realiza- tions; shaded regions show the sample-to-sample spread. The initial state is |1⟩, and time is in units of 1/J.
